content.title: Phoenix Contact in Japan 2026
content.subtitle: From Components to Resilient Automation Infrastructure
content.message: Japan’s 2026 demand is shifting toward automation, secure connectivity, data-center resilience, and energy-stable infrastructure.

metrics.label: Japan factory automation & industrial controls market, 2026

content.title: Japan Automation Tailwinds Are Quantifiable
content.subtitle: Factory automation spending is expanding from 2026 onward
content.message: Japan’s factory automation and industrial controls market is forecast to grow to USD 28.57B by 2031.

content.title: Control-System Modernization Is the Faster Layer
content.subtitle: Japan industrial automation and control systems are forecast at 12.1% CAGR
content.message: The broader control-systems market grows from USD 24.5366B in 2025 to USD 61.1875B by 2033.

content.title: Hardware Remains the Base; Services Are the Upside
content.subtitle: Japan automation revenue is still hardware-heavy, but services grow faster
keyPoints: Services are forecast to grow at 10.41% CAGR through 2031.
SME segment shows strong growth potential at 10.68% CAGR.

content.title: PLC, MES, and Edge: The Factory Data Stack Opportunity
content.subtitle: Connecting field devices to digital production systems
keyPoints: Field Level: Sensors, I/O, Terminal blocks, Connectors
Control Level: PLCnext, Controllers, Industrial PCs
Network Level: Managed switches, PoE, Industrial Ethernet
Operations Level: SCADA, MES, Edge analytics

content.title: Industrial Connectivity: Durable Category, APAC-Led Demand
content.subtitle: Market expansion led by Asia-Pacific growth
keyPoints: Industrial connectors projected to grow to USD 11.65B by 2035.
APAC accounts for over 44.5% of 2025 revenue.
Circular connector segment remains a dominant share at 31.5%.

content.title: Data Centers: A Visible 2026 Japan Growth Vector
content.subtitle: Targeting redundancy and secure operations
keyPoints: Focus on Data Center Japan 2026 Exhibition (Booth 2A-09).
Key solutions: Power Supply, UPS, and Surge protection.
Targeting secure networking and mission-critical reliability.

content.title: Cybersecure Industrial Networking Becomes Core
content.subtitle: Cybersecurity is now part of OT infrastructure procurement
keyPoints: CRA compliance and IEC 62443 standard compatibility.
FL MGUARD security product family as a central pillar.
Integration of secure industrial switches (Unmanaged, PoE, Managed).

content.title: Phoenix Contact Global Context: Recovery and Localization
content.subtitle: Global investment supports Japan-market credibility
keyPoints: Expected FY2025 turnover of EUR 3.3B with recovery trends.
Expansion of Vietnam site and Blomberg logistics (200,000 m3).
Localization efforts ensuring supply continuity for Japanese customers.

content.title: Expert Voice: Technology-Led Sustainability
content.subtitle: Strategic alignment with Japan's automation needs

quote.text: Despite all the challenges, we are convinced that technology is the key to a sustainable future.
quote.speaker: Ulrich Leidecker
quote.title: Chief Operating Officer, Phoenix Contact

content.title: Strategic Implications for Phoenix Contact Japan
content.subtitle: Prioritize solution bundles for high-growth sectors
body: Prioritize: Data-center redundancy and semiconductor automation bundles.
Scale: SME automation kits and connector replacement programs.
Maintain: Standalone terminal block and power supply sales.
Monitor: AI-native control use cases and new compliance demands.
